getting-started.cardSummary: Install the app from the MK App Store, sign in, pass the Macedonia location gate, and finish the coach tour.
getting-started.title: Set up Chisto.mk

blocks.text: Download Chisto.mk from the MK App Store: https://apps.apple.com/mk/app/chisto-mk/id6771892086. You can also use the download buttons on chisto.mk.

blocks.title: Before you install
items: Search the store for Chisto.mk and confirm the publisher matches our official listing.
Use Wi-Fi on a limited data plan. The first launch may download map tiles.
Keep iOS updated so camera, maps, and security patches stay current.

blocks.text: If the store page is unavailable, open chisto.mk in a desktop browser and follow the official App Store link.
Words like Home feed, Map tab, Report FAB, and report stages match the app so you always know which screen we mean.
Open the app and sign up with your phone number, email, name, and password. You must verify your phone with an SMS one-time code before the account is active.

blocks.title: What to prepare
items: A Macedonian mobile number you can read SMS on.
An email inbox you check for account notices.
A strong password you do not reuse on other services.

blocks.text: Chisto.mk is available in Macedonia. During sign up the app checks that your device location is inside the country. After that you land on the Home feed with nearby pollution sites and feed filters.

blocks.title: First steps on Home feed
items: Scroll the feed or tap feed filters such as All, Urgent, Nearby, and Top support.
Tap a site card to open photos, history, and Take action options.
Open the Map tab when you want pins and the heatmap view.

blocks.text: If you travel outside Macedonia, browsing may still work, but reporting and eco actions require you to be in the country.
After your first sign in, a coach tour walks you through the main tabs: Home feed, Map tab, Reports list, Report FAB, Events, and your profile avatar.

blocks.title: Main tabs
items: Home feed shows ranked site cards and filters.
Map tab shows pins, heatmap, and site detail sheets.
Reports lists your submissions with status chips.
Events lists cleanup and eco-action listings.
The Report FAB in the center starts the 4-step report wizard.

blocks.text: You can skip the coach tour anytime. Reopen it later from profile settings if you want a refresher.
You control push notifications in iOS Settings and inside the app inbox preferences. Our Privacy policy explains how personal data is processed when you report sites or join events.

blocks.title: Where to look
items: iOS: Settings, Notifications, Chisto.mk.
In app: open the notifications inbox and tap the preferences icon.
Review the eight notification groups before your first cleanup.

sign-in-and-verification.cardSummary: Sign in with phone and password, complete SMS OTP verification, and recover access when codes stop arriving.
sign-in-and-verification.title: Sign in and verification

blocks.text: Returning users sign in with the same phone number and password created at registration. The app formats Macedonian numbers automatically.

blocks.title: Sign in steps
items: Open Chisto.mk and tap Sign in.
Enter your phone number and password.
Tap Sign in and wait for the session to load.

blocks.text: Keep your device clock set to automatic. Large clock skew can break SMS code validation.
New accounts and some security flows send a one-time code by SMS. Enter the code before it expires. Codes are short lived and cannot be reused.

blocks.title: If the code does not arrive
items: Confirm airplane mode is off and you have signal.
Wait one minute, then tap Resend code.
Check that the phone number has no typos.
Disable SMS filtering apps that might hide sender messages.

blocks.text: Paste the code carefully. Autofill from Messages usually works on iOS.
Registration requires your device location to be inside Macedonia. Sign in itself does not repeat the gate every time, but reporting, eco actions, and some edits check location again.

blocks.title: When location is blocked
items: Enable Location Services for Chisto.mk while using the app.
Turn off mock location or developer GPS tools.
Move outdoors briefly so GPS can settle near tall buildings.

blocks.text: Use Forgot password on the sign in screen. You receive instructions by email or SMS depending on the flow active in your build.

blocks.title: Recovery checklist
items: Use the same email you registered with.
Open recovery links on the same device when possible.
Choose a new password that meets the app rules.

blocks.text: Sign out from profile settings before handing your phone to someone else. Draft reports stay on the device until you discard them or submit.

blocks.title: Good habits
items: Enable a screen lock on phones you carry daily.
Do not share SMS codes with anyone.
Update the app from the MK App Store when updates appear.

app-permissions.cardSummary: Allow camera, photos, location, notifications, and motion access so reporting, map pins, and event check-in work reliably.
app-permissions.title: App permissions

blocks.text: Chisto.mk asks for iOS permissions only when a feature needs them. Denying access blocks parts of the 4-step report wizard, Map tab accuracy, or event QR check-in.

blocks.title: Core permissions
items: Camera and Photos for Evidence stage photos.
Location while using the app for pins in Macedonia.
Notifications for report status and event updates.

blocks.text: You can change permissions later in iOS Settings. The app shows inline hints when something is missing.
Choose While Using the App. The Location stage needs a pin inside Macedonia. Nearby feed filters and map centering also use your position.

blocks.title: Fix location issues
items: Open Settings, Privacy and Security, Location Services, Chisto.mk.
Enable Precise Location when offered.
Restart the app after changing the setting.

blocks.text: The Evidence stage accepts up to five photos from the camera or library. Without access, you cannot complete a report.

blocks.title: Recommended settings
items: Allow camera access when prompted during your first report.
Allow photo library access to attach existing images.
Free storage space if uploads fail repeatedly.

blocks.text: Capture in daylight when safe. You can add library photos later in the same draft.
Push alerts cover report status, nearby reports, cleanup events, and event chat. You can fine tune eight notification groups inside the app even when push is allowed.

blocks.title: Enable alerts
items: Tap Allow when iOS asks during onboarding.
If you skipped it, open Settings, Notifications, Chisto.mk.
Open the in app inbox preferences to mute or snooze groups.

blocks.text: Open iOS Settings, scroll to Chisto.mk, and review each toggle. Motion or fitness data is not required for core reporting.

blocks.title: After you change settings
items: Force close and reopen Chisto.mk once.
Retry the action that failed, such as Report FAB or QR scan.
Check troubleshooting if errors persist.

offline-and-slow-networks.cardSummary: Save report drafts locally, queue uploads on weak signal, and avoid duplicate submissions while pending.
offline-and-slow-networks.title: Offline and slow networks

sections.title: How the app uses the network

blocks.text: Maps, photos, and submissions sync when connectivity returns. Some map tiles cache so you can browse lightly offline. Account actions usually need a live session.

blocks.title: Expectations
items: Draft fields save locally until you submit.
Large photos take longer on mobile data.
Event chat and check-in need a connection.

blocks.text: Pending uploads are normal briefly after switching from Wi-Fi to mobile data.
The 4-step report wizard saves progress as a draft. You can leave mid flow and resume from the Reports tab or the Report FAB sheet.

blocks.title: Draft tips
items: Finish Evidence and Details before you lose signal if possible.
Confirm the Location pin when you regain GPS.
Submit on Wi-Fi when photos are large.

blocks.text: Pending means the device is still sending data or the server has not confirmed receipt. Do not start a second report for the same issue while pending spins.

blocks.title: If you are stuck
items: Open the report summary and look for retry prompts.
Capture smaller photos if uploads fail repeatedly.
Wait on stable Wi-Fi before force closing the app.

blocks.text: Report credits are consumed on successful submit, not while a draft is still local.
International roaming can block background uploads. Finish uploads before flights when possible.

blocks.title: Travel tips
items: Reconnect to Wi-Fi before editing long descriptions.
Double check event reminders if time zones shift.
QR check-in needs live data at the venue.

blocks.text: Toggle cellular data for Chisto.mk in iOS Settings if roaming blocks only this app.
If the app crashes twice while uploading, pause and collect OS version and app version before contacting support. Reinstalling can clear local drafts.

blocks.title: Before you write to us
items: Restart the device once.
Confirm you are on the latest build from the MK App Store.
Capture screenshots of error text and blur personal details.

troubleshooting.cardSummary: Fix sign-in, SMS codes, map location, camera uploads, and know when to update or contact support.
troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting common issues

blocks.text: Most login issues come from connectivity, clock skew, or an outdated build. Work through the list before you assume the password is wrong.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Turn airplane mode off, wait, then reconnect.
Set date and time to automatic.
Request a fresh SMS code and paste carefully.
Update Chisto.mk from the MK App Store, then restart.

blocks.text: The Map tab and Location stage need location access. If the blue dot drifts, fix permissions before you file a report.

blocks.title: Checklist
items: Enable Location Services while using the app.
Open sky for a few seconds so GPS settles.
Disable mock location tools while using Chisto.mk.
Move the pin manually if GPS lands on the wrong block.

blocks.text: Pins must stay inside Macedonia. Drag the pin if GPS places you outside the border.
Large photos and weak networks do not mix. Give uploads time or capture a smaller photo when the queue keeps failing.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Confirm camera and photo library permission.
Free storage space on the device.
Switch to Wi-Fi for multi photo uploads.
Reopen a saved draft instead of starting over.

blocks.text: Avoid duplicating the same report while the first upload still shows pending.
You receive a limited pool of report credits. When all credits and the emergency allowance are used, the Report FAB shows a cooldown message.

blocks.title: Unlock more reports
items: Join eco-action and verify attendance at check-in.
Create an eco action as an organizer after passing the organizer quiz.
Wait for the emergency unlock timer if shown.

blocks.text: When nothing above works, collect a short description and reach out. Note your iOS version and app version.

blocks.title: Before you contact us
items: Restart the phone once.
Confirm the official MK App Store listing.
Describe which tab failed: Home feed, Map tab, Reports, or Events.

partnerships-for-organisations.cardSummary: How municipalities, NGOs, schools, and journalists can use Chisto.mk map data and events responsibly.
partnerships-for-organisations.title: Partnerships for municipalities, NGOs, and media

blocks.text: This article is for organisations that want to use public map data, coordinate cleanups, or explore collaboration. It is not legal advice.

blocks.title: Typical readers
items: Municipal environment desks planning field follow up.
NGOs mapping volunteer capacity near reported sites.
Schools hosting supervised eco actions.
Journalists verifying locations before publishing.

blocks.text: Student groups should work with a teacher or NGO contact before publishing events.
Residents file reports through the mobile app. Approved reports become public pollution sites on the map. Organizers can publish cleanup events and eco actions subject to moderation.

blocks.title: Public signals you can rely on
items: Site pins with photos and community history.
Event listings with RSVP and check-in data when organizers enable it.
Status labels such as Under review, Approved, and Declined on submissions.

blocks.text: Map pins are civic signals, not automatic legal findings. Use official channels for regulated enforcement.
Gather your goal, geography, and timeline. If you need bulk data, explain public benefit and how you will protect personal data.

blocks.title: Helpful attachments
items: Official email from an organisational domain when possible.
One paragraph on intended use of map or event information.
Preferred language: Macedonian, Albanian, or English.

blocks.text: Binding rules live in our Terms, Privacy policy, and data pages. The help centre explains product behavior for residents.

blocks.title: Where to read more
items: Use the data page for retention and rights paths.
Use the privacy page before processing identifiable information.
Link staff to the help hub so field teams share app vocabulary.

home-feed-and-sites.cardSummary: Browse the Home feed, use feed filters, open site cards, and start Take action flows from a pollution site.
home-feed-and-sites.title: Home feed and pollution sites

blocks.text: The Home feed is the default tab after sign in. It shows pollution sites ranked for your area with photos, status, and community activity.

blocks.title: What you see
items: Site cards with headline, location hint, and support counts.
Badges when a site needs attention or has new reports.
Your profile avatar in the header for quick access to settings.

blocks.text: Pull to refresh when you return from the field with new photos.
Tap feed filters above the list to change ranking. Filters include All, Urgent, Nearby, and Top support.

blocks.title: When to use each filter
items: All: balanced default ranking.
Urgent: new or unverified sites that need community attention.
Nearby: closest reports around your current location.
Top support: sites with the most community upvotes.

blocks.text: Nearby requires location permission. Enable it in Settings if the filter looks empty.
Tap a card to open the site detail sheet with photos, timeline, comments, and Take action options.

blocks.title: On the site page
items: Swipe photos and read the latest approved report text.
View history events such as new reports and cleanups.
Upvote or comment when you want to show support.

blocks.text: Take action opens options such as filing a new report, Join eco-action, or viewing linked cleanup events.

blocks.title: Common choices
items: Report again when you have fresh Evidence photos.
Join eco-action to RSVP to an existing cleanup.
Create eco action when you plan to host a new event.

blocks.text: Switch to the Map tab to see the same sites as pins. Toggle the heatmap to spot clusters of activity.

blocks.title: Map tab tips
items: Pinch to zoom until street names match the ground.
Tap a pin to open the same site sheet as the feed.
Use Recenter when you want to jump back to your position.

exploring-the-map.cardSummary: Navigate pins, toggle the heatmap, open site sheets, and decide when to file a new report.
exploring-the-map.title: Exploring the Map tab

blocks.text: The Map tab is a shared civic view of pollution sites in Macedonia. Each pin is a place where someone documented an environmental concern.

blocks.title: Quick orientation
items: Zoom until street names and landmarks match what you see outside.
Tap a pin to open photos, descriptions, and history.
Counts change as moderators approve new reports.

blocks.text: Toggle Show heatmap to highlight areas with more reported activity. Hide heatmap when you need a cleaner view for precise pin placement.

blocks.title: Using the heatmap
items: Look for hot zones when planning group cleanups.
Turn heatmap off before confirming a Location pin.
Combine with Nearby feed filter when scouting on foot.

blocks.text: Heatmap density reflects reported sites, not official enforcement priority.
A site sheet summarises community submissions. It supports transparency, not assigning blame to individuals.

blocks.title: What you can rely on
items: Photos and text come from contributors and moderators.
Timestamps show recency when displayed.
Take action links reporting, events, and comments in one place.

blocks.text: Do not treat map pins as legal proof on their own.
Use the map while stationary or as a passenger. Keep awareness of traffic and private property boundaries.

blocks.title: Good habits
items: Prefer daylight for first visits to unfamiliar edges.
Step back and capture from a distance if a path feels unsafe.
Respect no entry signs even when rubbish is visible beyond them.

blocks.text: Do not use the Map tab while driving. Pull over safely first.
Open the Report FAB when you can add fresh evidence or correct a pin. Duplicates without new facts slow moderation.

blocks.title: Before you start
items: Gather photos and note what changed since the last visit.
Confirm you have report credits available.
Complete all four stages: Evidence, Details, Location, Review.

verifying-sites-in-the-field.cardSummary: Plan a safe visit, compare the pin to what you see, and file a new report when evidence clearly changed.
verifying-sites-in-the-field.title: Verifying pollution sites on the ground

blocks.text: Open the site from Home feed or Map tab, note the pin and photos, then plan daylight access from a public vantage.

blocks.title: Checklist
items: Charge your phone and expect weak signal in some valleys.
Tell a colleague where you go if you work alone.
Avoid private land unless you already have permission.

blocks.text: Stand where the map suggests and scan for the same cues in photos: kerb lines, trees, buildings, or shoreline bends.

blocks.title: When to report again
items: The hazard moved materially since the last photos.
The pin sits on the wrong block or riverbank.
You can document a risk that was not visible earlier.

blocks.text: Small GPS offsets happen near cliffs and tall buildings. Large mismatches deserve a careful new report.
Use the Report FAB and complete Evidence with up to five photos. Add Details that explain what changed since prior visits.

blocks.title: Field photo tips
items: Start wide for context, then move closer to the problem.
Include scale cues when size is unclear.
Stay out of traffic, unstable ground, and unknown liquids.

blocks.text: On the Location stage, drag the pin to the exact spot inside Macedonia. Tap Confirm location before Review.

blocks.title: Pin quality
items: GPS helps, but confirm visually on the map.
Describe access limits in Details when paths are blocked.
Submit only when Review shows all requirements met.

blocks.text: If GPS is outside Macedonia, drag the pin manually before you confirm.
Do not enter unstable piles, fast water, or industrial yards without training. Treat residents courteously.
If you discover immediate danger to life, use official emergency channels first, then document when safe.

report-a-site.cardSummary: Use the Report FAB and complete Evidence, Details, Location, and Review to submit a report that moderators can verify.
report-a-site.title: Report a pollution site

blocks.text: Tap the Report FAB from any main tab. If you have a saved draft, choose Continue draft or start fresh. Confirm you have report credits before you begin.

blocks.title: Before you start
items: Stay safe around traffic, unstable ground, and unknown liquids.
Check report credits on your profile if the FAB shows a cooldown.
Allow camera and location permissions when prompted.

blocks.text: Reporting requires your location to be in Macedonia.
Add up to five photos that show the polluted area clearly. At least one photo is required to continue.

blocks.title: What to capture
items: Wide shot for context, then closer frames of waste, stains, or debris.
Daylight and a steady hand help moderators verify faster.
Tap the stage help icon for examples any time.

blocks.text: Never enter private property without permission. Capture from a public vantage when possible.
Choose a category and write a short headline anyone could understand. Add optional severity, description, and cleanup effort notes.

blocks.title: Required fields
items: Category that matches what you saw.
Headline like a news title, not an essay.
Optional description for access, smell, color of water, or landmarks.

blocks.text: Only category and title are required to reach the Location stage.
Move the pin to the exact pollution site inside Macedonia. Zoom until buildings or shoreline match what you see on the ground, then tap Confirm location.

blocks.title: Pin tips
items: Drag the pin if GPS is offset near tall buildings.
The map cannot pan outside Macedonia.
Return to this stage from Review if the pin needs a fix.

blocks.text: Double-tap Recenter to jump to your GPS position when you are inside Macedonia.
Review summarizes photos, Details, and the pin. Submit sends the report for moderation. Status starts as Under review until moderators approve or decline it.

blocks.title: After submit
items: Track status in the Reports tab.
Approved reports link to a public pollution site.
Declined reports include a reason when moderators provide one.

report-statuses-and-drafts.cardSummary: Understand Under review, Approved, and Declined labels, and resume saved drafts from Reports or the Report FAB.
report-statuses-and-drafts.title: Report statuses and drafts

blocks.text: Each submission moves through moderation before it affects the public map. Status chips appear on the Reports tab and in notifications.

blocks.title: Common statuses
items: Under review: moderators are checking evidence and location.
Approved: the report linked to a public pollution site.
Declined: the submission did not meet guidelines.

blocks.text: Already reported may appear when your submission duplicates an existing open site.
Open the Reports tab to search and filter your submissions. Tap a row for the full timeline and linked site.

blocks.title: Reports tab actions
items: Filter by status such as Under review or Approved.
Open approved reports to see the public site page.
Read moderator notes on declined items when available.

blocks.text: Pull to refresh the Reports tab after you receive a Report status notification.
Leaving the wizard saves a draft locally with your current stage. Draft chips show photo count on the Reports list.

blocks.title: Resume a draft
items: Tap the draft row in Reports.
Or tap Report FAB and choose Continue draft.
Discard draft from the resume sheet if you want to start over.

blocks.text: Drafts survive app restarts but may be lost if you reinstall or clear app data.
Weak networks may leave a submit pending. Wait on Wi-Fi before you discard or duplicate the same report.

blocks.title: If submit fails
items: Reopen the draft and tap Submit again on Review.
Check troubleshooting for camera or upload errors.
Contact support if the same error repeats after an update.

blocks.text: Successful submits use report credits. Join eco-action or create an eco action to unlock more when you hit the limit.

blocks.title: See your balance
items: Open profile and find the Report credits card.
Read the cooldown message when all credits are spent.
Verify event attendance to restore capacity faster.

join-a-cleanup-event.cardSummary: Browse eco actions on the Events tab, RSVP with Join eco-action, and prepare for QR check-in on the day.
join-a-cleanup-event.title: Join a cleanup event

blocks.text: Open the Events tab to browse cleanup and eco-action listings in Macedonia. Read organiser notes, difficulty, and the meeting pin before you travel.

blocks.title: Before you travel
items: Confirm start time in local time and expected duration.
Check terrain and kit requirements in the listing.
See whether registration closes early.

blocks.text: Events tab filters help you narrow by date and status when the list is long.
Tap Join eco-action on an open listing to RSVP. You must be in Macedonia for join flows that create commitments on the map.

blocks.title: After you join
items: Add the event to your calendar once you commit.
Enable Cleanup events notifications for last minute changes.
Cancel promptly if you cannot attend so headcounts stay accurate.

blocks.text: Joining and verifying attendance can unlock additional report credits.

sections.title: From a site Take action sheet

blocks.text: On a pollution site, Take action may list linked eco actions near that pin. This helps you join cleanups where community reports already exist.

blocks.title: Site linked events
items: Open the site from Home feed or Map tab.
Tap Take action and pick Join eco-action when listed.
Review the pin matches the site you care about.

blocks.text: Follow the organiser safety plan, respect private property, and keep the group aligned with public access rules.

blocks.title: On site
items: Wear gloves or high vis gear when the listing asks for them.
Supervise children closely around roads and water.
Ask before photographing people, vehicles, or homes.

blocks.text: For emergencies, use official emergency channels alongside any in-app notes.
Most events use QR check-in at arrival. Open the event detail on the day and follow Event check-in and chat for scan steps and chat rules.

blocks.title: Day of reminders
items: Arrive with battery and mobile data or venue Wi-Fi.
Open the event screen before you queue at the organiser desk.
Keep Event chat notifications on for venue updates.

event-check-in-and-chat.cardSummary: Scan the organiser QR at arrival, confirm attendance, and use event chat for venue updates.
event-check-in-and-chat.title: Event check-in and chat

blocks.text: Open the event you joined from the Events tab. Confirm the meeting pin, start time, and any organiser messages in event chat.

blocks.title: Prepare your phone
items: Charge above 30 percent before you travel.
Allow camera access for QR scanning.
Enable Event chat notifications for route changes.

blocks.text: At the venue the organiser displays a QR code. Tap Check in on the event screen and scan the newest code shown.

blocks.title: Scan steps
items: Open the joined event detail.
Tap Check in or Scan QR.
Point the camera at the organiser screen, not a photo shared earlier.
Wait for the success confirmation before you walk away.

blocks.text: QR codes refresh automatically. Scan the live code on the organiser device.
Verified attendance can unlock report credits and points. If scan fails, ask the organiser to refresh the QR session.

blocks.title: Common scan errors
items: Wrong event QR: confirm you opened the correct listing.
QR expired: ask the organiser to show a fresh code.
Already used: wait while the organiser regenerates the session.

blocks.text: Stand in good light and hold the phone steady a forearm length from the screen.
Event chat lets organisers and volunteers coordinate before, during, and after the shift. Messages notify through the Event chat preference group.

blocks.title: Chat etiquette
items: Keep messages short and on topic for the venue.
Do not share personal phone numbers publicly in chat.
Report abusive messages through trust and safety tools.

blocks.text: Follow organiser instructions for zones, waste bags, and photo rules. After the event, upload after photos when the flow invites them.

blocks.title: Wrap up
items: Thank hosts through event chat when appropriate.
Avoid faces or licence plates unless organisers allow them.
Leave the listing a fair rating if the app prompts you.

hosting-a-cleanup-event.cardSummary: Pass the organizer quiz, publish an eco action, run QR check-in, and moderate event chat on the day.
hosting-a-cleanup-event.title: Host a cleanup event

blocks.text: First time hosts complete the organizer quiz before Create eco action unlocks. Questions cover safety, moderation, and responsible use of the platform.

blocks.title: Quiz tips
items: Read each question carefully. Sessions expire if you pause too long.
Reload the quiz if you see a session error message.
Certification stays on your profile after you pass.

blocks.text: You must be in Macedonia to create an eco action.
From Events or Take action on a site, tap Create eco action. Fill required fields such as title, meeting pin, schedule, and capacity.

blocks.title: Before you publish
items: Confirm land access with owners or municipalities when required.
Set realistic difficulty and kit expectations.
Link the event to a pollution site when the cleanup targets a report.

blocks.text: New listings may show Under review until moderators approve them.
Events can be Under review, Approved, or Declined. Edit and resubmit when moderators ask for changes.

blocks.title: Dashboard labels
items: Under review: volunteers cannot join yet.
Approved: Join eco-action opens for everyone.
Declined: read the banner and tap Edit and resubmit.

blocks.text: Open the organiser dashboard and display the live QR for volunteers. Invalidate previous QR codes if a screenshot leaked.

blocks.title: On site leadership
items: Brief everyone on hazards, buddy pairs, and first aid.
Refresh QR before each wave if codes expire.
Use event chat for short updates instead of long threads.

blocks.text: Close the loop with thank you notes, after photos, and honest feedback. Organizer approved events can earn profile points.

blocks.title: Wrap up
items: Mark the event complete when the shift ends.
Log municipal follow ups separately from volunteer chat.
Archive chat decisions so future hosts learn from your runbook.

your-profile-and-settings.cardSummary: Edit your avatar, language, home location, notification prefs, and account controls from your profile.
your-profile-and-settings.title: Your profile and settings

blocks.text: Tap your avatar from Home feed or any main tab. Profile shows points, weekly rankings, report credits, and activity summaries.

blocks.title: Profile sections
items: Points and level card with recent reasons.
Report credits balance and cooldown hints.
Links to settings, notifications, and help.

blocks.text: Update display name, avatar photo, and bio from profile edit. Keep information respectful and avoid sharing private contact details publicly.

blocks.title: Edit steps
items: Tap Edit profile on your profile screen.
Choose a square photo that reads well at small size.
Save and confirm the preview looks correct.

blocks.text: Use a neutral photo if you represent an organisation publicly.
Switch interface language among system default, English, Macedonian, and Albanian. Set home location to tune feed ranking when you are away from GPS.

blocks.title: Location settings
items: Home location must be inside Macedonia.
Update it when you move to a new city for daily browsing.
Map pins for reports still use the Location stage GPS pin.

blocks.text: Settings holds password change, notification shortcuts, legal links, and sign out. Sign out before sharing your device.

blocks.title: Common tasks
items: Open notification preferences from settings or the inbox.
Replay the coach tour if you skipped onboarding.
Review Terms and Privacy links before you invite minors.

blocks.text: Block user from another profile or from an abusive comment thread. Blocked people cannot interact with you in app social features.

blocks.title: When to block
items: Use block for harassment or spam directed at you.
Blocking is reversible from settings when you choose.
Report serious threats through Contact as well.

points-rankings-and-credits.cardSummary: Earn points for approved reports and events, climb weekly rankings, and manage report credits.
points-rankings-and-credits.title: Points, rankings, and report credits

blocks.text: Points reward constructive participation. Common reasons include report approved, eco action approved, and verified event attendance.

blocks.title: Typical point events
items: Report approved after moderation.
Eco action approved for organizers.
Verified check-in at a cleanup you joined.

blocks.text: Points are gamification only. They do not replace official environmental enforcement.
Profile shows weekly rankings for reports, eco actions, and related activity. Rankings reset on a schedule shown in the app.

blocks.title: Ranking tips
items: Quality reports beat rushed duplicates.
Join eco-action and check in to boost event participation.
Compare your rank with friends to stay motivated.

blocks.text: Weekly rankings subtitle in profile mentions reports, eco actions, and more.
Report credits limit how many standard reports you can submit in a period. The profile card shows remaining credits and retry timers.

blocks.title: When credits run out
items: Read the cooldown modal on the Report FAB.
Join eco-action and verify QR attendance to unlock more.
Create an eco action after organizer quiz certification.

blocks.text: Emergency allowance may appear once per cycle. Watch the retry timer in the modal.
Levels group your lifetime points. System notifications may announce achievements or milestones.

blocks.title: Stay informed
items: Leave the System notification group enabled for achievements.
Open the points card to read recent reasons.
Share wins in event chat, not public phone numbers.

blocks.text: Moderators decline gaming behavior such as duplicate spam or fake check-ins. Play fair so rankings stay meaningful for everyone.

blocks.title: Avoid
items: Mass low quality reports to farm points.
Sharing QR screenshots to fake attendance.
Harassing users who rank above you.

account-and-data.cardSummary: Protect your login, understand what we store, and use the right pages for privacy and data requests.
account-and-data.title: Your account, privacy, and data requests

blocks.text: Your Chisto.mk account connects reports, eco actions, notification preferences, and profile activity across Macedonia.

blocks.title: What may be linked to you
items: Phone number, email, and password hash for sign in.
Map pins and descriptions from reports you submit.
RSVP status, check-in records, and event chat for events you join.

blocks.text: Public site pages show community content, not your private contact details.
Treat the account like a mailbox for civic issues. Strong access controls protect you and the map data others rely on.

blocks.title: Everyday hygiene
items: Use a unique password or your OS password manager.
Keep screen lock enabled on phones you carry daily.
Sign out before handing a device to someone else.

blocks.text: You can ask what we hold, correct mistakes, export a copy, or request deletion, subject to lawful exceptions such as moderation records.

blocks.title: How to start a request
items: Use in app account settings wherever available.
Email support if a control is missing or the flow errors.
Expect identity checks when requests are sensitive.

blocks.text: Binding legal wording lives on dedicated pages. Read them when you need certainty beyond these guides.

blocks.title: Where to look
items: Privacy explains what we collect and why.
Terms explain acceptable use of the platform.
Cookies explains optional website analytics.

notifications-in-the-app.cardSummary: Manage the inbox, eight notification groups, snooze controls, and OS push settings together.
notifications-in-the-app.title: Notifications in the app

blocks.text: Tap the bell icon to open the notifications inbox. Similar alerts collapse into expandable notification groups by day.

blocks.title: Inbox actions
items: Tap a row to open the related site or event.
Expand a group to read every message in the stack.
Mark a group as read or archive it when done.

blocks.text: Event chat stacks by conversation so one tap opens the full thread.
Preferences organize alerts into eight groups you can mute or snooze independently.

blocks.title: The eight groups
items: Site updates
Report status
Upvotes
Comments
Nearby reports
Cleanup events
Event chat
System, including achievements and welcome messages

blocks.text: Snooze Event chat the night before a cleanup if you need sleep, then re-enable it on event morning.
Your phone always wins. If push is off at the iOS level, Chisto.mk cannot override that. Use both OS settings and in-app toggles.

blocks.title: Where to look
items: iOS: Settings, Notifications, Chisto.mk.
In app: inbox preferences icon for group switches.
Profile settings for quick links to the same sheet.

blocks.text: Snooze a group temporarily from the preferences sheet when you need quiet hours.
Report status notifications fire when moderators move a submission to Approved or Declined. Cleanup events notify RSVP changes and reminders.

blocks.title: Recommended defaults
items: Keep Report status on for your own submissions.
Enable Nearby reports when you volunteer locally.
Enable Cleanup events and Event chat on days you join eco-action.

blocks.text: SMS one-time codes and some security messages are required for sign in. Marketing style messages only arrive when you clearly opt in.

blocks.title: Stay in control
items: Keep email reachable to recover access if you lose your phone.
If SMS codes stop, see sign in troubleshooting.
Read the Privacy policy for processing details.

trust-safety-and-moderation.cardSummary: Why moderation exists, how to contribute respectfully, block users, and where binding legal text lives.
trust-safety-and-moderation.title: Trust, safety, and moderation

blocks.text: Chisto.mk publishes civic environmental signals. Moderation reduces spam, protects people from harassment, and keeps the map readable for partners in Macedonia.

blocks.title: What reviewers look for
items: Evidence that matches the Location pin and time window.
Respectful language without personal attacks.
Duplicates folded into the same site when that keeps the story clearer.

blocks.text: Reports and eco actions show Under review until moderators decide. Approved content appears publicly. Declined content includes guidance when moderators provide it.

blocks.title: Status meanings
items: Under review: human review in progress.
Approved: visible on Home feed, Map tab, or Events.
Declined: does not meet guidelines. Edit and resubmit when allowed.

blocks.text: Assume good intent from neighbours and organisers. Focus on conditions you can photograph or describe factually.

blocks.title: Community norms
items: No doxxing: avoid posting private addresses for people instead of sites.
No hate speech or coordinated brigading.
Do not upload imagery that sexualises minors or glorifies self harm.

blocks.text: If tempers rise, pause and capture facts only. Moderators prioritise safety over speed.
Block user on profiles or comment threads when someone targets you. For serious threats, contact us with screenshots and URLs.

blocks.title: Escalation paths
items: Block first for persistent harassment in app.
Use Contact for illegal content or imminent harm.
Organisers can remove disruptive event chat participants per house rules.

blocks.text: This article summarises behavior expectations. Binding rules sit in the legal pages linked below.

blocks.title: Where to read more
items: Privacy explains data categories and retention.
Terms explain acceptable use and enforcement levers.
The data page lists rights requests for account holders.
